subject: Chemistry
title: Element 118: The End of the Table?
description: One-minute skill: Element 118: The End of the Table?.

hook.text: The periodic table currently ends at element 118. Scientists are already trying to make 119 and 120.

buildup.text: Every element past uranium (92) was created artificially. The heavier they get, the harder they are to make.

discovery.text: Element 118 took thousands of hours of bombardment to produce just three atoms.

twist.text: Elements 119 and 120 would start a new row. But current accelerators may not be powerful enough.

climax.text: Japan, Russia, and Germany are racing to be first. The periodic table is still being written.

punchline.text: Row 8 hasn't started yet. The table has no last page.

quiz.question: Why is it so hard to create elements beyond 118?
options: Current particle accelerators may not be powerful enough
Scientists have lost interest in heavy elements
There are no target materials left to use
